critic_raw:
ASSESSOR 1:
Post-treatment outcome contamination is the primary issue. Model scores drive support assignment; support changes re-enrollment; re-enrollment is used to measure model quality. Cross-year comparison adds cohort and policy confounds. Verdict: critique_wins.
defender_raw:
ASSESSOR 2:
Improved feature set and missing-data handling are genuine methodological contributions. But the paper cannot establish 'more accurate' because the evaluation is post-treatment. The 34% vs. 22% capture rate reflects a combination of model quality and intervention targeting, which are confounded. Verdict: critique_wins.
adjudication_raw:
ASSESSOR 3:
Three assessors agree: post-treatment outcome, cross-year comparison, and evaluation circularity.

SYNTHESIS:
Clean consensus on critique_wins. All three must-find issues identified. The paper's accuracy claim is not supported by this evaluation design.

ETD SPECIFICATION:
- Measure: Retrospective scoring of both models on the same pre-program cohort (e.g., the prior year's students before any structured early-alert intervention was available) and comparison of top-quintile non-return capture rates
- Success criterion: No material difference in capture rates on the pre-program cohort — intervention assignment explains the live advantage
- Failure criterion: New model shows higher capture on pre-program cohort, confirming genuine predictive superiority

empirical_test.measure: Both models scored on same pre-program cohort; compare top-quintile non-return capture rates
empirical_test.success_criterion: No material difference in capture rates on pre-program cohort
empirical_test.failure_criterion: New model shows substantially higher capture on pre-program cohort
